tropics.outlook: Tropical cyclone formation is not expected during the next 7 days.

spots.name: Narragansett Town Beach
spots.notes: Lacks shape, often closes out. Sunken barge + old pier near the North Pavilion. The small-swell default.

spots.name: Point Judith — East
spots.notes: The Aves + East of the Lighthouse. Rock bottom, rocks are the hazard. East/south face split is derived, not sourced.

spots.name: Point Judith — South
spots.notes: True point break, steep left and right; long lefts south of the lighthouse. Block Island notch at 195-205 is why the window is split. NE is offshore here and onshore at Point Judith East — the two faces of the headland want opposite winds, which is the defining contrast of this coast.

spots.name: K38 / K39
spots.notes: Mushy left, mostly fed through the breakwater gap. Size range unsourced.

spots.name: Matunuck
spots.notes: Covers Deep Hole, Trestles and The Point — three cobble reefs sharing one swell window (135-183, 197-215). Low tide matters: Deep Hole needs it, and all three get slow and fat at high. Boots year-round.

rationale: Every spot is size-limited — the biggest surf reaching any of them today is 1.51 ft.
Warm Winds calls it 1–2 ft; we make it 1.51 ft. We agree on the size.
Our “worth it” bar (35/100) assumes you want a wave with some push. At 1.51 ft it's a longboard day, not a nothing day — if you log, go anyway.
